request.question: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose’s superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

hypothesis.intervention: replace sucrose with trehalose as the cryoprotectant in the freezing medium
hypothesis.threshold: at least 15 percentage points higher than the standard DMSO protocol
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All six core fields are explicitly stated in the hypothesis with a concrete intervention, named cell line, defined comparator, named outcome, numeric threshold, and a biological mechanism.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: trehalose DMSO cryopreservation HeLa viability trypan blue
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose’s superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

references.title: Trehalose-based cryopreservation of mammalian cells
references.relevance_note: Covers trehalose as a cryoprotectant in mammalian systems.

literature_qc.explanation: Papers using trehalose as a cryoprotectant exist in the literature, primarily in the context of cell preservation and lyophilization. No paper was found combining trehalose with HeLa cells specifically using trypan blue viability as the primary endpoint.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

risks.mitigation: Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.

risks.description: Control strategy may miss process-specific artifacts in handling or reagent preparation.
risks.mitigation: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

plan.objective: To determine the effect of trehalose as a cryoprotectant on the post-thaw viability of HeLa cells compared to the standard DMSO protocol.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be cryopreserved using two different cryoprotectants: trehalose and DMSO. Each treatment will be performed in triplicate to ensure statistical validity. Post-thaw viability will be assessed using trypan blue exclusion assay, and results will be compared to determine if trehalose provides at least a 15 percentage point increase in viability over DMSO.
controls: HeLa cells cryopreserved using 10% DMSO in freezing medium.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Reagent Preparation
Prepare 10% (w/v) trehalose solution by dissolving 1 g of trehalose (Sigma-Aldrich, T9531) in 10 mL of sterile phosphate-buffered saline (PBS, pH 7.4).
Equipment: Pipettes, Sterile 15 mL centrifuge tubes, Vortex mixer
Reagents: 1 g trehalose (Sigma-Aldrich, T9531), 10 mL sterile PBS (pH 7.4)
Expected outcome: Trehalose solution is clear and homogenous.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Reagent Preparation
Prepare 10% (v/v) DMSO solution by diluting 1 mL of DMSO (Sigma-Aldrich, D8418) in 9 mL of sterile freezing medium (FBS with 10% DMSO).
Equipment: Pipettes, Sterile 15 mL centrifuge tubes
Reagents: 1 mL DMSO (Sigma-Aldrich, D8418), 9 mL sterile freezing medium (FBS with 10% DMSO)
Expected outcome: DMSO solution is clear and homogenous.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 48 h | Cell Culture
Culture HeLa cells in a T-75 flask with DMEM (Gibco, 11965-092) supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin-streptomycin at 37°C, 5% CO2 until 80% confluence is reached (approximately 48 h).
Equipment: T-75 cell culture flask, Incubator (Thermo Scientific, Heracell 150i)
Reagents: DMEM (Gibco, 11965-092), 10% FBS, 1% penicillin-streptomycin
Expected outcome: HeLa cells reach 80% confluence.

DAY 1 | 15 min | Cell Harvesting
Detach HeLa cells using 0.25% trypsin-EDTA (Gibco, 25200-056) for 5 min at 37°C. Neutralize with complete medium and centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature.
Equipment: Centrifuge (Eppendorf 5424), Pipettes, Sterile 15 mL centrifuge tubes
Reagents: 0.25% trypsin-EDTA (Gibco, 25200-056), Complete medium
Expected outcome: Cells are detached and form a pellet after centrifugation.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Cryopreservation
Resuspend the cell pellet in either 10% trehalose solution or 10% DMSO solution at a density of 1 × 10^6 cells/mL. Aliquot 1 mL into cryovials (Nunc, 377248).
Equipment: Pipettes, Cryovials (Nunc, 377248)
Reagents: 10% trehalose solution, 10% DMSO solution
Expected outcome: Cryovials contain 1 mL of cell suspension at 1 × 10^6 cells/mL.

DAY 1 | 2 h | Controlled Freezing
Place cryovials in a controlled-rate freezer (Thermo Scientific, Revco ULT) and cool at a rate of -1°C/min until -80°C is reached, then transfer to liquid nitrogen for storage.
Equipment: Controlled-rate freezer (Thermo Scientific, Revco ULT)
Expected outcome: Cryovials are stored at -80°C.

DAY 2 | 5 min | Thawing Cells
Retrieve cryovials from liquid nitrogen and thaw rapidly in a 37°C water bath until just thawed (approximately 2-3 min).
Equipment: 37°C water bath (Thermo Scientific)
Expected outcome: Cells are thawed and ready for viability assessment.

DAY 2 | 2 h | Cell Recovery
Transfer thawed cells to 10 mL of complete medium and incubate at 37°C, 5% CO2 for 2 h to allow recovery.
Equipment: T-25 cell culture flask, Incubator (Thermo Scientific, Heracell 150i)
Reagents: Complete medium
Expected outcome: Cells recover and adhere to the flask.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Viability Assessment
Perform trypan blue exclusion assay by mixing 10 µL of cell suspension with 10 µL of 0.4% trypan blue (Gibco, 15250-061) and counting viable cells using a hemocytometer.
Equipment: Hemocytometer, Pipettes
Reagents: 0.4% trypan blue (Gibco, 15250-061)
Expected outcome: Viability percentage calculated from cell counts.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Calculate the post-thaw viability percentage for each treatment group (trehalose and DMSO) and compare using a two-tailed t-test with a significance level of p < 0.05.
Equipment: Computer with statistical software (e.g., GraphPad Prism)
Expected outcome: Statistical significance determined between trehalose and DMSO treatments.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Replicate Verification
Verify that each treatment group has been performed in triplicate and confirm that the results meet the threshold of at least 15 percentage points higher viability for trehalose compared to DMSO.
Equipment: Computer with statistical software (e.g., GraphPad Prism)
Expected outcome: Replicate results confirm statistical validity of findings.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: Trehalose will provide superior cryoprotection compared to DMSO in HeLa cells.
Post-thaw viability can be accurately measured using trypan blue exclusion assay.
The freezing and thawing protocols are optimized for HeLa cells.
decision_criteria: Proceed if trehalose-treated cells show at least 15 percentage points higher viability than DMSO-treated cells.
Confirm that all triplicate samples yield consistent results within ±5% of the mean viability.
Statistical significance (p < 0.05) is achieved in the comparison of viability between treatments.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same batch of trehalose for all experiments.
Maintain consistent cell passage number (≤10 passages) for all HeLa cells used.
Calibrate the controlled-rate freezer before use to ensure accurate cooling rates.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 15 percentage points higher than the standard DMSO protocol improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
